arguments.task: Open official Zendesk developer and pricing documentation pages and extract evidence for: one-line product description, API authentication methods, API types and breadth, rate limits, pricing/access tier for API use, and whether Zendesk offers an official MCP server. Prefer canonical first-party pages that load successfully in browser. Return the supporting page URLs and short quotes or summaries from each page. Also check official docs for OAuth, API tokens, basic auth, APIs overview, rate limits, pricing/plans, and any official MCP page.

input.thought: Open official Zendesk sources and extract canonical evidence for structured research fields.

raw_result.description: Customer service and help desk software for managing support across channels.
raw_result.auth_notes: Zendesk’s security and authentication docs for the REST APIs document OAuth access tokens, API tokens, and basic authentication.
raw_result.gating_reason: Zendesk API use is tied to paid Zendesk plans; official pricing and rate-limit docs reference Suite plan tiers such as Team, Growth, Professional, Enterprise, and Enterprise Plus for API capacity.

api_surface.breadth: Official developer docs expose broad REST APIs across Zendesk products and platform areas, including API reference sections for Support/Ticketing and other Zendesk product surfaces.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Zendesk Model Context Protocol server was verified from the gathered first-party evidence, so this is recorded as not verified as an official offering.

raw_result.blocker: Requires a paid Zendesk plan/account to use the production API, even though the public developer docs and auth methods are documented.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official rate-limit docs describe plan-based API limits and rate-limit headers; gathered evidence included Suite plan examples of roughly 200 requests/minute for Team, 400 for Growth/Professional, 700 for Enterprise, and 2500 for Enterprise Plus, with 429 behavior when exceeded.
raw_result.notable_signals: Public developer portal is extensive, with dedicated API basics, security/authentication, rate-limit, and API reference sections; plan-based API limits and a High Volume API add-on signal mature production API support.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The structured field mcp_exists is not directly supported by a verified first-party MCP page in the gathered evidence; absence of evidence is not definitive proof that no official MCP exists.
